a:r.a:t: The provider system abstracts away the underlying LLM services so agents do not care whether they are talking to OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, or any other provider. The providers directory has 6 files: factory, llm_factory, circuit_breaker, output_limits, and dependency_checks. At the core, AgentProviderResolver caches bundles by a 6-tuple of provider, model, and capabilities. Each agent can override its LLM via the AGENT_NAME_CHAT_MODEL environment variable. Token caps follow a chain: request-level overrides agent-level, which overrides global, which overrides Ollama defaults. A ProviderBundle packages three models together: the chat model for conversation, the judge model for grading relevance, and the embeddings model for vector search. Embedding dimensions are 768 for Nomic and 1536 for Ada-002. The circuit breaker is the reliability story. It uses a 20-sample sliding window and requires at least 6 samples before it can trip. If the error rate exceeds 50 percent or 3 consecutive failures occur, the circuit opens for a 30-second cooldown period. During this time, no requests go to that provider. The fallback chain is important: if an agent's provider trips the circuit breaker, RuntimeService automatically degrades to basic mode with a different provider. If even that fails, the system returns a graceful degraded message rather than an error. This multi-layer resilience is critical for production reliability.
